message:
Running command: # Great! All BFMs just include the smoke_bfm. But our runner adds -DDEFINE too.
# When both the bfm file and -DDEFINE are provided, there will be duplicate defines.
# Let me check the runner - it adds -DTEST_SEQ_XIP when running xip_directed_bfm
# but the bfm file already `define TEST_SEQ_XIP
# iverilog should handle this gracefully (duplicate define = no error usually)

# The REAL issue: the runner passes the -DDEFINE flag which causes a duplicate.
# Let's see if that causes any issues... usually it's fine.

# But the runner also includes the BFM tb file through the tb filelist.
# Wait - the tb filelist only includes smoke_bfm and fw_boot.
# The runner uses: -f design_root/tb/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f
# which includes: smoke_bfm.v and fw_boot.v (both)
# For the xip_directed scenario, we compile:
# - xip_directed_bfm.v (which defines TEST_SEQ_XIP and includes smoke_bfm.v)
# - PLUS the tb filelist (which also includes smoke_bfm.v)
# = DOUBLE INCLUDE of smoke_bfm!

cat /workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/tb/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

cat /workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/tb/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f"}
